rules: [P1] Calculate exact temporal deltas and validate against specific clinical thresholds before initiating interventions.
[P1] Link the justification for state-changing actions directly to the specific temporal threshold or clinical interval defined in the instruction.
[P1] Distinguish between 'data retrieval failure' and 'clinical absence' before triggering state-changing actions.
[P1] Distinguish between informational assessment and operational execution tasks.
[P1] Retrieve all relevant time-series data for target metrics before applying window-based filters.
[P1] Explicitly validate conditional logic outcomes as a formal step before initiating state-changing actions.
[P1] Terminate execution flows immediately if a conditional prerequisite is not met.
[P1] Verify order existence and clinical conflicts across all statuses before declaring absence.
[P1] Categorize missing data by type: 'normal', 'missing', 'not performed', or 'outside temporal window'.
[P1] Identify all required data points and cross-reference against protocol decision trees before evaluating eligibility.
[P1] Perform comprehensive categorical searches (classes, synonyms, variants) rather than keyword-specific searches.
[P1] Explicitly map clinical findings to protocol-defined actionable categories.
[P1] Avoid interventions exceeding explicit scope or lacking primary dependencies.
[P1] Define the reference 'current' timestamp explicitly before filtering time-series data.
[P1] Do not terminate the episode until all conditional state-correction actions are confirmed complete.
[P1] Execute conditional instructions sequentially, ensuring mandatory remediation steps are prioritized.
[P1] Perform preliminary data reconnaissance: filter invalid records and establish temporal anchors before declaring unavailability.
[P1] Terminate workflows gracefully when essential inputs are missing rather than assuming baseline values.
[P1] Treat 'absence of record' or 'absence of history' as a valid clinical state to trigger expiration-based or new-order criteria.
[P1] Triangulate device/status by querying disparate clinical domains.
[P1] Validate whether isolated data points fall within the requested window before declaring the task unfeasible.
[P1] Prioritize temporal sorting to isolate the most recent snapshot and normalize date comparisons.
[P1] Prioritize clinical indications over temporal thresholds when dual-criteria exist.
[P1] Prioritize state reconciliation: discontinue mismatched orders before creating new ones to maintain cardinality.
